strengths: The agent's communication was mostly professional, indicating a good foundation in communication skills.
The agent was able to identify that the international team would be best placed to handle the issue, showing some understanding of the escalation process.
The agent transferred the customer to the international team in a timely manner, indicating efficiency in handling the call.
improvements: The agent needs to verify transfer details with the customer, such as date and time of the transfer, recipient's account number, and transfer amount, before transferring them to another team.
The agent should provide an estimated timeframe for when the transfer should arrive in the recipient's account, even if it's a general estimate.
The agent must take ownership of the issue and ensure it's properly escalated or investigated before transferring the customer to another team.

rephrasing.original: The international team would be best placed to tell you when the transfer should arrive.
rephrasing.better: I can provide you with a general estimate of when the transfer should arrive, but the international team will be able to give you a more accurate timeframe. Would you like me to note this down for you?

rephrasing.original: I'll transfer you to the international team.
rephrasing.better: Before I transfer you to the international team, can I just confirm a few details with you? Can you please confirm the date and time of the transfer, the recipient's account number, and the transfer amount? I'll also make a note of this for the international team to follow up on.

rephrasing.original: I'll transfer you to the international team.
rephrasing.better: I'm going to transfer you to the international team, but I want to make sure that we've properly escalated this issue. Can I just summarize what we've discussed so far and what we're expecting the international team to investigate? This way, we can ensure that we're providing the best possible support for you.
